description: Newmont is a U.S.-based global mining company focused on gold production, with meaningful copper, silver, zinc, and lead exposure across a multi-continent portfolio.

overview.title: Business Profile
paragraphs: Newmont is one of the world’s largest gold producers, operating a global portfolio of mines, development projects, and exploration assets. Its core business is extracting and processing gold, with copper increasingly relevant as a strategic byproduct and reserve base rather than just a line item buried in the geological footnotes.

The company’s portfolio spans North America, South America, Australia, Papua New Guinea, and Africa, giving it broad operational diversification but also exposure to the full menu of mining-sector risks: commodity cycles, permitting, water stewardship, tailings management, community agreements, and jurisdiction-specific tax and royalty regimes.

overview.title: Product And Market Exposure
paragraphs: Newmont’s primary product exposure is gold mining operations, supported by copper mining operations and additional production of silver, zinc, and lead. Gold remains the economic center of gravity, while copper gives the company some connection to electrification demand without turning it into a pure energy-transition metals story.

The company reports a large attributable gold reserve base and substantial copper reserves, making reserve quality, mine sequencing, cost inflation, and capital allocation central to any assessment of its long-term competitive position.

overview.title: Registry Relevance
paragraphs: For Free The World, Newmont is best treated as a materials-sector extraction company rather than a technology platform. The relevant comparison surface is not software openness but the degree to which mining operations, supply chains, environmental controls, safety practices, and community commitments are transparent, auditable, and substitutable.

Potential alternative mappings should focus on recycled metals, lower-impact sourcing standards, public-interest mineral traceability, circular-economy materials strategies, and demand-reduction pathways where applicable. There is no obvious repo technology wave mapping yet, which is probably correct unless the registry adds a dedicated materials, mining, or circularity taxonomy.
